comprehension_gate.instruction: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION: Before any branch creation, file edits, or commands, read this entire prompt and all required attachments in full. Then present a structured comprehension summary to the Lead Architect (or the Co-Lead reviewer under the DEC-12 peer-review lattice) covering: (a) milestone objective in your own words (b) work items with 1 sentence each (c) files to READ and files to WRITE (d) exact structural requirements for docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (e) ORACLE strategy for this DOCS-ONLY synthesis milestone (f) risks or ambiguities (specifically: synthesis-only milestone with zero production or test file reads; only the audit artifact itself and the two skipped-test sites are in scope) (g) EXACT deliverable structure, recited VERBATIM from this prompt: - branch to create: feature/p5-task7-ea5-synthesis from main at a3419e9 - files to WRITE: docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md only - docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md must retain exactly these 6 top-level sections in this order: 1. ## Coverage Map 2. ## Stale Test Inventory 3. ## Assertion Quality Findings 4. ## Boundary Violations 5. ## Prioritized Gap Report 6. ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis - preserve sections 1-4 and the EA Index rows EXACTLY as they appear on main — EA-5 adds ZERO content to sections 1-4 - append exactly one new EA Index row: | EA-5 | synthesis (sections 5-6) | `feature/p5-task7-ea5-synthesis` | `a3419e9` | Entry 50 | - populate ## Prioritized Gap Report by REPLACING the exact line `Deferred to EA-5 synthesis.` with the full synthesis content specified in this prompt - populate ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis by REPLACING the exact line `Deferred to EA-5 synthesis.` with the full synthesis content specified in this prompt - append Entry 50 for this milestone using current ledger numbering Then STOP and WAIT for explicit Lead Architect (or Co-Lead reviewer) approval. Do NOT create the branch, write files, or run commands until the reviewer confirms that your recitation is correct.

milestone.title: Task 7 / EA-5: Prioritized Gap Report + Pre-existing Skip Analysis Synthesis
milestone.model_rationale: Task 7 closing synthesis. Requires holistic judgment across eight service clusters' findings (policy_agent, assistant_orchestrator, semantic_router, ui_gateway, ui_shell, shared, launcher, integration) with prioritization discipline; no new code reads, pure synthesis of the existing canonical artifact plus two skip sites.
milestone.predecessor: Main HEAD a3419e9 is Task 7 EA-4 (Entry 49) merge 1f4aa20 plus two unrelated follow-up commits (4be41f7 archiving the EA-4 queue file, and a3419e9 fixing the Vikunja autostart script). Neither follow-up commit touches docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md or docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. The canonical Task 7 artifact on main now contains sections 1-4 populated for all eight service clusters (policy_agent, assistant_orchestrator, semantic_router, ui_gateway, ui_shell, shared, launcher, integration) and sections 5-6 as stubs reading exactly "Deferred to EA-5 synthesis."
milestone.objective: Close Task 7 by populating sections 5 and 6 of docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md through synthesis of the existing sections 1-4 and direct inspection of the two pre-existing skipped tests. EA-5 is limited to: (a) the canonical audit artifact itself, (b) docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md, and (c) read-only inspection of the two skipped-test sites for Section 6 evidence. EA-5 must NOT modify, re-read, or re-litigate any sections 1-4 content — that content is accepted on main. EA-5 must append ledger Entry 50 to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. No production files or test files may be modified.

artifact_state.item: docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md on main contains populated sections 1-4 across eight service clusters.
Section 5 (## Prioritized Gap Report) on main is a single-line stub: "Deferred to EA-5 synthesis."
Section 6 (## Pre-existing Skip Analysis) on main is a single-line stub: "Deferred to EA-5 synthesis."
EA-5 replaces each stub with its synthesized content and leaves sections 1-4 unchanged byte-for-byte except for the single new EA Index row.

milestone.scope:
Synthesis input (READ-ONLY, for analytical reasoning — do not modify): - docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md (all of sections 1-4 across eight service clusters) - docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md (governing standard for HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW prioritization) - docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (Entries 39-49 for Task 7 historical context)
Skip-site evidence (READ-ONLY — do not modify): - shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py (contains two pytest.skip sites at approximately lines 78 and 98, both guarding symlink creation on systems lacking elevated privileges)
Synthesis output (WRITE): - docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md (Section 5 body, Section 6 body, and EA Index append only) - docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (Entry 50 append only)
Explicitly out of scope: - No re-reading of production source files under services/, shared/, launcher/. - No re-reading of test files other than the two skip sites in shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py. - No running of the test suite. - No changes to any file other than the two listed docs files.

work_items.item: Read docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md sections 1-4 end-to-end and inventory every distinct finding recorded across the eight service clusters. Do not modify any content under sections 1-4.

For each finding inventoried in WI-1, classify severity as HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW according to the rubric in this prompt's prioritization_rubric block. Produce deduplicated cross-service groupings where a pattern (e.g. stale NPU nomenclature, missing fail-closed error-code assertions, exact-threshold boundary gaps, `constants.py` UNCOVERED status) recurs across clusters.

Author ## Prioritized Gap Report body per the section_5_contract in this prompt. Keep the section strictly bounded by the eight service clusters already documented on main — introduce no new service or subsystem not already named in sections 1-4.

Inspect the two pytest.skip sites in shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py (approximately lines 78 and 98) and record, for each: the test function name, the exact skip reason string, the production behavior each test is covering, and a disposition recommendation (FIX / REMOVE / KEEP).

Author ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis body per the section_6_contract in this prompt. Do not introduce any skipped-test discussion for tests that are not in this explicit two-test set — the continuation XML locks the Section 6 scope at exactly two skipped tests.

Preserve the existing EA Index rows (EA-1, EA-1 Correction, EA-2, EA-3, EA-4) exactly as they appear on main. Append exactly one new EA Index row: `| EA-5 | synthesis (sections 5-6) | \`feature/p5-task7-ea5-synthesis\` | \`a3419e9\` | Entry 50 |`.
Append Entry 50 to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md using current main numbering, declaring Task 7 COMPLETE.
Enforce strict DOCS-ONLY hygiene: stage and commit only docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. No other staged files are acceptable.

If mid-session the synthesis cannot be completed without quality degradation (e.g. ambiguous prioritization for a cluster, insufficient context to make a KEEP/FIX/REMOVE determination on a skip), invoke Tier 3 fail-safe: produce a clearly labeled partial synthesis, record PARTIAL disposition in Entry 50, and recommend a re-scope rather than forcing a low-quality close-out.

deliverable.primary_artifact: Modify docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md on main-derived state. Preserve sections 1-4 and the existing EA Index rows exactly as-is. Replace the stub content of sections 5 and 6 with synthesized findings.
deliverable.header_contract:
Preserve the current top block exactly as it exists on main: - Title exactly: # Test Audit Findings - Keep: **Audit Type:** DOCS-ONLY qualitative audit — no production or test file modifications **Task:** P5-Task-7 **Governance Reference:** `docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md` ### EA Index
Preserve the existing EA Index rows exactly: - | EA-1 | policy_agent | `feature/p5-task7-ea1-policy-agent-audit` | `88b330d` | Entry 39 | - | EA-1 Correction | policy_agent | `feature/p5-task7-ea1-audit-correction` | `488b198` | Entry 40 | - | EA-2 | assistant_orchestrator, semantic_router | `feature/p5-task7-ea2-ao-sr-audit-correction` | `6ab1ece` | Entry 41 | - | EA-3 | ui_gateway, ui_shell | `feature/p5-task7-ea3-ui-gateway-ui-shell-audit` | `85cae8b` | Entry 48 | - | EA-4 | shared, launcher, tests/integration | `feature/p5-task7-ea4-shared-launcher-integration-audit` | `b858919` | Entry 49 |
Append exactly one new row: - | EA-5 | synthesis (sections 5-6) | `feature/p5-task7-ea5-synthesis` | `a3419e9` | Entry 50 |
deliverable.exact_structure:
docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md must continue to contain exactly these 6 top-level sections in this exact order: 1. ## Coverage Map 2. ## Stale Test Inventory 3. ## Assertion Quality Findings 4. ## Boundary Violations 5. ## Prioritized Gap Report 6. ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis
Under sections 1-4, the service subheadings on main are already: - ### policy_agent - ### assistant_orchestrator - ### semantic_router - ### ui_gateway - ### ui_shell - ### shared - ### launcher - ### integration
EA-5 must NOT add, rename, remove, or reorder any service subheading under sections 1-4. EA-5 must NOT add any text to sections 1-4 other than preserving them byte-for-byte from main. The only changes to docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md are: - the appended EA-5 row in the EA Index table - the replacement of "Deferred to EA-5 synthesis." in Section 5 with the Section 5 synthesized content - the replacement of "Deferred to EA-5 synthesis." in Section 6 with the Section 6 synthesized content
deliverable.section_5_contract:
Under ## Prioritized Gap Report, produce synthesized remediation items organized into three priority tiers using the exact subheading order and text:
### HIGH Priority
### MEDIUM Priority
### LOW Priority
Each item under each priority tier is a bullet whose body begins with the source service cluster(s) in brackets (e.g. `[policy_agent]`, `[policy_agent, semantic_router]`, `[cross-service]`), followed by a one-sentence description of the remediation action, followed by a colon and a short justification citing the relevant section(s) by name (e.g. "Stale Test Inventory", "Boundary Violations") — do NOT cite sections by number.
Deduplicate patterns that recur across clusters into a single cross-service item whenever the remediation action is substantially identical (e.g. stale NPU nomenclature across policy_agent, assistant_orchestrator, and semantic_router should collapse to one `[cross-service]` item referencing all affected clusters).
Every item must reference findings that already appear in sections 1-4 of the canonical artifact on main. EA-5 must NOT introduce findings that are absent from sections 1-4.
If any priority tier has no items, write exactly one line under its subheading: No HIGH priority items identified. (Adjust for MEDIUM and LOW accordingly.)
End the section with a synthesis summary paragraph (2-4 sentences) titled:
### Synthesis Summary
The Synthesis Summary must: - state the total remediation-item count across all tiers - name the dominant remediation themes (e.g. stale naming, missing error-code assertions, exact-threshold boundary gaps, `constants.py` coverage) - state whether Task 7 acceptance criteria are met or whether Tier 3 fail-safe is invoked - do NOT speculate about timelines, owners, or downstream tasks — remediation scheduling is outside Task 7 scope.
deliverable.section_6_contract:
Under ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis, synthesize an analysis for exactly the two pre-existing skipped tests in shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py (near lines 78 and 98; both guarded by symlink-privilege skip calls).
The section must contain exactly these two subsections in this order:
### Skip 1 — shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py (first symlink skip site)
### Skip 2 — shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py (second symlink skip site)
Each subsection must record: - Test function name (verbatim from the test file) - Skip reason string (verbatim from the pytest.skip call) - Production behavior covered (what production path does the test exercise when not skipped?) - Platform sensitivity (Windows vs. POSIX implications) - Disposition — exactly one of the following labels in bold: **KEEP** — rationale: ... (short paragraph) **FIX** — rationale: ... (short paragraph) **REMOVE** — rationale: ... (short paragraph)
After both subsections, add a summary subsection titled:
### Skip Disposition Summary
The Skip Disposition Summary must: - state each test's disposition (KEEP / FIX / REMOVE) - state the collective recommendation (e.g. both KEEP, or a mix) - do NOT introduce any additional skipped tests — Section 6 is locked to exactly these two sites
If EA-5 concludes that the two skip sites require production or test file changes to resolve, Section 6 must recommend the change in narrative form only — EA-5 must NOT modify the test file or any production file.
deliverable.prioritization_rubric:
Use this rubric when assigning HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW in Section 5:
HIGH: - Any finding under ## Boundary Violations that is a real boundary-correctness defect (not a preserved "no violations" sentence). - Any fail-closed path where a critical error code or security-relevant assertion is missing (e.g. missing error_code field assertion on fail-closed PA/AO paths, missing JWT validation negative case). - Any untested exact-threshold boundary at a critical decision surface (e.g. escalation floor 0.50 in policy_agent, PGOV leakage 0.85 in assistant_orchestrator, dual-gate thresholds in semantic_router). - Any stale nomenclature or stale architectural assumption that could mislead a future engineer into reintroducing retired hardware paths (e.g. ADR-011 NPU residues post-retirement).
MEDIUM: - Coverage gaps labeled PARTIAL or THIN on modules with non-trivial logic (e.g. boot.py THIN, entrypoint.py PARTIAL, runtime_config.py PARTIAL, launcher/__main__.py PARTIAL). - Weak assertions flagged under ## Assertion Quality Findings that are not fail-closed-critical (e.g. field-presence-only checks in integration groups, circuit-breaker shallow assertions). - Redundant or partially-overlapping test files that add maintenance burden without unique coverage (e.g. test_adjudicator.py vs test_hybrid_adjudicator.py Group I overlap in policy_agent).
LOW: - `constants.py` UNCOVERED-implicit status across multiple clusters (low ROI — constants are exercised transitively). - Stale naming that is not safety- or correctness-relevant (e.g. leftover comment references to retired model names that do not appear in active test identifiers). - Indirect-only coverage flags on modules whose behavior is adequately exercised via their callers (e.g. schemas/car.py indirect-only, session_panel.py indirect-only).
When a finding could plausibly fall into two tiers, choose the HIGHER tier if any of the following applies: the finding touches a fail-closed path, a security assertion, an exact decision threshold, or an architectural-retirement violation. Otherwise choose the lower tier.
deliverable.ledger_contract:
Append Entry 50 to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md using the existing ledger format.
Required content: - Title: Entry 50 - Task 7/EA-5: Prioritized Gap Report + Pre-existing Skip Analysis Synthesis (COMPLETE) - Date: 2026-04-21 or actual execution date - Branch: feature/p5-task7-ea5-synthesis - Predecessor: Entry 49 - Task 7/EA-4: Shared + Launcher + Integration Audit (COMPLETE) - Type: AUDIT / DOCS-ONLY / SYNTHESIS - Disposition: COMPLETE (or PARTIAL if Tier 3 fail-safe triggered — in that case include a clear re-scope recommendation) - Summary: EA-5 closed Task 7 by synthesizing sections 1-4 across eight service clusters into the Prioritized Gap Report and analyzing the two pre-existing symlink-privilege skipped tests for the Pre-existing Skip Analysis; sections 1-4 preserved byte-for-byte; Task 7 audit is now complete. - Files: docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md only - Task 7 Closure Declaration: State explicitly that Task 7 is now COMPLETE and that the Prioritized Gap Report is the authoritative remediation backlog for any follow-on test-quality work.
In Entry 50 summary and key findings: - do not use numbered section references such as Section 4, 5.3, or 5.4 - use the exact section names (## Prioritized Gap Report, ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis, ## Boundary Violations, etc.) when cross-referencing - do not re-litigate accepted sections 1-4 content - do not create follow-on tasks in the ledger; the Prioritized Gap Report is the handoff, scheduling is outside Task 7

negative_constraints.item: DO NOT modify any file under services/, shared/, launcher/, or tests/.
DO NOT modify any file under docs/scheduled/.
DO NOT add any content to sections 1-4 of docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md — they must remain byte-for-byte identical to main except for the single new EA Index row.
DO NOT alter, reformat, or reorder any existing service subheading under sections 1-4.
DO NOT alter the existing EA Index rows for EA-1, EA-1 Correction, EA-2, EA-3, or EA-4.
DO NOT add top-level sections beyond the 6 required sections.
DO NOT rename any of the 6 required top-level sections.
DO NOT add numbered prefixes to section headers (## 5. Prioritized Gap Report is forbidden).
DO NOT introduce remediation items in Section 5 that are not traceable to findings already recorded in sections 1-4.
DO NOT introduce skipped-test analyses in Section 6 for tests that are not in the explicit two-site set in shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py.
DO NOT propose fixes, patches, or code changes inline — Section 5 items and Section 6 dispositions are recommendations only.
DO NOT speculate about remediation scheduling, owners, or downstream task identifiers — scheduling is outside Task 7 scope.
DO NOT run pytest, coverage tooling, or install dependencies.
DO NOT touch unrelated pre-existing untracked files in the worktree.

context_notes.note: docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md is the governing standard for this audit. Use its scope definitions, marker policy, boundary rules, and qualitative coverage expectations when calibrating HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW priority.

EA-5 is a synthesis milestone. Sections 1-4 are accepted on main and must not be re-litigated. If EA-5 identifies what it believes is a missing finding in sections 1-4, that belongs in a follow-on remediation task — NOT in sections 1-4 of this artifact.
docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md on main is the canonical shared Task 7 artifact. EA-5 must extend it in-place by populating only sections 5 and 6 and by appending one EA Index row.

Oversight includes an independent Co-Lead completion review under the DEC-12 peer-review lattice. HIGH priority items must meet the rubric criteria (fail-closed, security, critical threshold, or architectural-retirement residue). Inflating MEDIUM items to HIGH to appear thorough is a quality defect. Collapsing HIGH items to MEDIUM to appear conservative is also a quality defect.

note.title: Skip scope is locked at two

context_notes.note: The continuation XML (Section 6 contract) locks Section 6 scope at exactly two pre-existing skipped tests. These are the two symlink-privilege skips in shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py. If EA-5 encounters additional skipped tests during incidental reading, it must NOT extend Section 6 to include them. Out-of-scope skipped tests (if any) should be recorded in Entry 50 as an observation, not added to Section 6.

The worktree may contain unrelated untracked artifacts (e.g. tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/, tools/fleet_observability/*.flag, scheduled state.json edits). Do not touch, stage, rename, or delete them. They are outside this milestone.

If the synthesis cannot be completed without quality degradation (e.g. ambiguous severity for an entire cluster, insufficient evidence to make a KEEP/FIX/REMOVE determination on a skip), invoke Tier 3 fail-safe: deliver an honest partial synthesis covering the tiers and skips for which the evidence is strong, record PARTIAL disposition in Entry 50, and recommend a re-scope (e.g. EA-5a Prioritized Gap Report only; EA-5b Skip Analysis only). Given this is Task 7's closing milestone, forcing a weak close-out is the worst outcome.

note.title: No follow-on task scheduling in this milestone

context_notes.note: The Prioritized Gap Report IS the handoff. It enumerates what remediation work exists and its priority. It does NOT schedule that work, assign it, or create follow-on Vikunja tickets. Any scheduling is a Lead Architect decision that lives outside Task 7.

gate.criteria: No Python files may be created or modified in this milestone.

Do not run pytest as a standard gate for this slice. Section 6 evidence comes from read-only inspection of shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py at the two skip sites. If a targeted read-only command is used to confirm a skip reason string verbatim, report it explicitly.

gate.command: Manual synthesis completion against sections 1-4 and the two skip sites
gate.criteria: All eight service clusters' findings in sections 1-4 are represented or explicitly deduplicated under cross-service items in Section 5. Both skip sites in shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py are analyzed in Section 6 with verbatim skip reason strings. If a Tier 3 fail-safe is invoked, clearly label which clusters or which skip did NOT reach a quality synthesis in Entry 50 PARTIAL disposition.

The top title remains exactly # Test Audit Findings. The document still contains the same 6 required top-level sections in the same order. Sections 1-4 contain the same eight service subheadings in the same order as on main, with content byte-for-byte preserved. The existing five EA Index rows remain intact and exactly one EA-5 row is appended. Sections 5 and 6 no longer contain the literal string "Deferred to EA-5 synthesis." — that stub is fully replaced.

gate.command: Review Section 5 structure and rubric compliance
gate.criteria: ## Prioritized Gap Report contains exactly ### HIGH Priority, ### MEDIUM Priority, ### LOW Priority subheadings in that order, followed by ### Synthesis Summary. Every Section 5 bullet begins with a service cluster bracket prefix and references a finding from sections 1-4. No numbered section references (no "Section 4", no "5.3") appear anywhere in Section 5. HIGH-tier assignments meet the rubric criteria; no inflation or deflation is evident on spot-check.

gate.command: Review Section 6 structure and skip scope
gate.criteria: ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis contains exactly ### Skip 1, ### Skip 2, and ### Skip Disposition Summary subheadings in that order. Each skip subsection records a verbatim skip reason string, the production behavior covered, platform sensitivity, and one of KEEP / FIX / REMOVE in bold. Section 6 analyzes exactly the two shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py skip sites — no additional skipped tests are introduced.

Entry 50 exists and matches the required title, date, predecessor (Entry 49), type (AUDIT / DOCS-ONLY / SYNTHESIS), and disposition. Entry 50 does not use numbered section references. Entry 50 explicitly declares Task 7 COMPLETE. If Tier 3 fail-safe is invoked, Entry 50 carries PARTIAL disposition and a clear re-scope recommendation.

If the synthesis cannot be completed without degraded quality, STOP, leave a clearly labeled partial report, record PARTIAL status in Entry 50, and recommend a re-scope instead of forcing a weak close-out. EA-5 is Task 7's closing milestone — honest partial is strictly better than forced weak close-out.

verification.instruction: The Lead Architect will verify this milestone using terminal commands only. Your final EA response must report results that these commands can confirm.

commands.expected: Single EA-5 synthesis commit on the feature branch
commands.command: Select-String -Path docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md -Pattern "^# Test Audit Findings$","^### EA Index$","^## ","^### policy_agent$","^### assistant_orchestrator$","^### semantic_router$","^### ui_gateway$","^### ui_shell$","^### shared$","^### launcher$","^### integration$","^### HIGH Priority$","^### MEDIUM Priority$","^### LOW Priority$","^### Synthesis Summary$","^### Skip 1","^### Skip 2","^### Skip Disposition Summary$"
commands.expected: Shows the preserved multi-EA header, the 6 required top-level sections, all eight preserved service subheadings under sections 1-4, the four new Section 5 subheadings, and the three new Section 6 subheadings.
No output — both stubs are fully replaced.
commands.command: Select-String -Path docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md -Pattern "Entry 50","Type: AUDIT / DOCS-ONLY / SYNTHESIS","Disposition: (COMPLETE|PARTIAL)","Task 7 (is now |)COMPLETE" | Select-Object -First 8
commands.expected: Ledger contains contract-compliant Entry 50 with Task 7 closure declaration.

commit_template.message:
Task 7 EA-5: synthesize Prioritized Gap Report + Pre-existing Skip Analysis
- Populated ## Prioritized Gap Report with HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW tiers synthesized from sections 1-4 across eight service clusters - Populated ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis with KEEP/FIX/REMOVE dispositions for both shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py symlink skip sites - Preserved sections 1-4 byte-for-byte; appended EA-5 row to the EA Index - Added Entry 50 in Post-Operational Maturation Ledger declaring Task 7 COMPLETE
